name: Mujaddara
recipeInstructions:
1. Start cooking 3/4 cup brown rice or reserve the same amount of uncooked bulghur.
2. Alternatively, start cooking 1 cup lentils.
3. Dice and brown 1 large onion in a large amount of olive oil (less than 6 tbsp).
4. After 30 minutes of lentil cooking, add the cooked rice or drained bulghur, the onion, 1 tsp cumin (or twice as much with coriander), pepper, and salt.
5. Simmer for another 15 minutes.
6. Brown (caramelize) another diced onion.
7. Add the caramelized onion to the top of the lentil/grain mixture and let sit for 10 minutes.
The dish has a good consistency. I've made this twice in the past week from George Lasalle's "Orphanides: My Middle Eastern Cooking."
